file_name: Principal Life Insurance Company - Broker Dealer Marketing and Servicing Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Broker Dealer represents and warrants that it is a broker dealer registered with the SEC under the 1934 Act, a member in good standing of FINRA, and is registered as a broker dealer under state law to the extent required in order for it or those persons who are registered with it and licensed as insurance producers ("Registered Representatives") to provide the services described in this Agreement. Broker Dealer agrees to abide by all rules and regulations of FINRA, including its Conduct Rules, and to comply with all applicable state and federal laws and the rules and regulations of authorized regulatory agencies affecting the sale and servicing of Annuity Contracts.
perturbation.changed_text: Broker Dealer represents and warrants that it is a broker dealer registered with the SEC under the 1934 Act, a member in good standing of FINRA, and is registered as a broker dealer under state law to the extent required in order for it or those persons who are registered with it and licensed as insurance producers ("Registered Representatives") to provide the services described in this Agreement. Broker Dealer agrees to abide by all rules and regulations of FINRA, but is not required to comply with state and federal laws and the rules and regulations of authorized regulatory agencies affecting the sale and servicing of Annuity Contracts.
perturbation.explanation: By stating the Broker Dealer is 'not required to comply with state and federal laws,' there is an internal contradiction with section 3, which discusses that Broker Dealer and Registered Representatives need to be properly registered, licensed and appointed to solicit applications for and service Annuity Contracts, thus requiring compliance with state and federal laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Issuer and Distributor appoint Registered Representatives to solicit and procure applications for the sale of and service of Annuity Contracts. This appointment is not exclusive and only extends to those jurisdictions in which the Annuity Contracts have been approved for sale and in which Broker Dealer and Registered Representatives are properly registered, licensed and appointed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Broker Dealer is 'not required to comply with state and federal laws,' which directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text that the Broker Dealer and Registered Representatives must be 'properly registered, licensed and appointed' in order to solicit applications. This creates a clear and direct conflict because proper registration, licensing, and appointment necessitate compliance with state and federal laws. The change creates an internal contradiction with section 3.

perturbation.original_text: Broker Dealer will use commercially reasonable efforts to train and instruct its Registered Representatives not to make recommendations to an applicant to purchase an Annuity Contract in the absence of reasonable grounds to believe that the purchase is suitable for such applicant, in accordance with the suitability and disclosure requirements of the 1934 Act, FINRA Conduct Rule 2310, and any state insurance law or regulation governing the offer and sale of Annuity Contracts, including any state law or regulation governing sales to the public in general (e.g. consumer protection laws or regulations, unfair trade practices, annuity disclosure regulations, etc.) or to senior citizens, as the same may be amended or interpreted from time to time. Broker Dealer will use commercially reasonable efforts to determine that each transaction is completed with a Registered Representative's report indicating suitability, including any required and necessary customer information, and is subjected to a review process in compliance with FINRA Conduct Rule 3010, as the same may be amended or interpreted from time to time. Each application shall be approved by one of Broker Dealer's registered principals, in accordance with all applicable FINRA rules.
perturbation.changed_text: Broker Dealer will train and instruct its Registered Representatives to make recommendations to an applicant to purchase an Annuity Contract, regardless of whether the purchase is suitable for such applicant, and irrespective of the suitability and disclosure requirements of the 1934 Act, FINRA Conduct Rule 2310, and any state insurance law or regulation governing the offer and sale of Annuity Contracts, including any state law or regulation governing sales to the public in general (e.g. consumer protection laws or regulations, unfair trade practices, annuity disclosure regulations, etc.) or to senior citizens, as the same may be amended or interpreted from time to time. Broker Dealer will use commercially reasonable efforts to determine that each transaction is completed with a Registered Representative's report indicating suitability, including any required and necessary customer information, and is subjected to a review process in compliance with FINRA Conduct Rule 3010, as the same may be amended or interpreted from time to time. Each application shall be approved by one of Broker Dealer's registered principals, in accordance with all applicable FINRA rules.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement of suitability ('regardless of whether the purchase is suitable for such applicant, and irrespective of the suitability and disclosure requirements of the 1934 Act, FINRA Conduct Rule 2310, and any state insurance law'), it now contradicts the last statement which states that 'Each application shall be approved by one of Broker Dealer's registered principals, in accordance with all applicable FINRA rules.', as FINRA rules require the determination of suitability. Therefore, the Broker Dealer principals would be unable to follow all FINRA rules, because the former portion of the perturbed section is breaking FINRA rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Broker Dealer will train and instruct its Registered Representatives to make recommendations to an applicant to purchase an Annuity Contract, regardless of whether the purchase is suitable for such applicant, and irrespective of the suitability and disclosure requirements of the 1934 Act, FINRA Conduct Rule 2310, and any state insurance law or regulation governing the offer and sale of Annuity Contracts, including any state law or regulation governing sales to the public in general (e.g. consumer protection laws or regulations, unfair trade practices, annuity disclosure regulations, etc.) or to senior citizens, as the same may be amended or interpreted from time to time. Broker Dealer will use commercially reasonable efforts to determine that each transaction is completed with a Registered Representative's report indicating suitability, including any required and necessary customer information, and is subjected to a review process in compliance with FINRA Conduct Rule 3010, as the same may be amended or interpreted from time to time. Each application shall be approved by one of Broker Dealer's registered principals, in accordance with all applicable FINRA rules.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that recommendations can be made 'regardless of whether the purchase is suitable for such applicant, and irrespective of the suitability and disclosure requirements'. This directly contradicts the requirement that 'Each application shall be approved by one of Broker Dealer's registered principals, in accordance with all applicable FINRA rules.', because FINRA rules mandate suitability assessments. Approving unsuitable applications would violate FINRA rules, creating a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Broker Dealer shall ensure that the prospectus delivery requirements under the 1933 Act and all other applicable securities and insurance laws, rules and regulations are met and that delivery of any prospectus for Annuity Contracts will be accompanied by delivery of the prospectus for the underlying mutual funds, and, where required by state law, the Statement of Additional Information for the underlying mutual funds. Issuer or Distributor shall inform Broker Dealer of those states that require delivery of a Statement of Additional Information with the prospectus on initial sale. Broker Dealer is responsible for prospectus delivery requirements only on initial sale. Issuer and Distributor will be responsible for prospectus delivery annually after the original sale.
perturbation.changed_text: Broker Dealer shall not be required to ensure that the prospectus delivery requirements under the 1933 Act and all other applicable securities and insurance laws, rules and regulations are met and that delivery of any prospectus for Annuity Contracts will be accompanied by delivery of the prospectus for the underlying mutual funds, and, where required by state law, the Statement of Additional Information for the underlying mutual funds. Issuer or Distributor shall inform Broker Dealer of those states that require delivery of a Statement of Additional Information with the prospectus on initial sale. Broker Dealer is responsible for prospectus delivery requirements only on initial sale. Issuer and Distributor will be responsible for prospectus delivery annually after the original sale.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the Broker Dealer is 'not required to ensure that the prospectus delivery requirements under the 1933 Act,' there is an internal contradiction with section 10, which explicitly requires the Broker Dealer 'to maintain all books and records relating to the servicing and sale of Annuity Contracts or interests therein required under the 1934 Act, and any applicable rules promulgated thereunder, and applicable securities or insurance laws of any states.' Without meeting the prospectus delivery requirements under the 1933 act, they would be in violation of the record keeping requirements mandated in section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Broker Dealer agrees to maintain all books and records relating to the servicing and sale of Annuity Contracts or interests therein required under the 1934 Act, and any applicable rules promulgated thereunder, and applicable securities or insurance laws of any states.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Broker Dealer is 'not required to ensure that the prospectus delivery requirements under the 1933 Act,' directly contradicting the requirement in the contradicted text that the Broker Dealer 'agrees to maintain all books and records relating to the servicing and sale of Annuity Contracts or interests therein required under the 1934 Act, and any applicable rules promulgated thereunder, and applicable securities or insurance laws of any states.' Compliance with the 1933 Act prospectus delivery requirements is essential for maintaining compliant books and records as required by the 1934 Act and applicable state laws.
